title: Habenaria helleborina
taxonomy: Habenaria helleborina is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the genus Habenaria. The genus Habenaria is a group of terrestrial orchids that are found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. Habenaria helleborina specifically is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It is classified as a terrestrial orchid that thrives in shaded, moist environments.
uses: Habenaria helleborina is a species of orchid used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. intro: Habenaria helleborina, also known as Helleborine, is a terrestrial orchid native to Asia and Europe. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria helleborina include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
